💡 Java 17 — sealed types close the inheritance graph with `permits`.

✅ Only listed subtypes may extend the sealed class/interface

✅ Each permitted subtype must be `final`, `sealed`, or `non-sealed`

✅ `non-sealed` is the escape hatch — inheritance opens again below that branch

#Java #Java17

💡 Java 14+ switch expressions — `switch` returns a value, and the compiler makes sure you didn't forget a case.

✅ Old switch: forget `break` and execution falls through to the next case

✅ New `case X -> "..."`: one line per case, returns a value, no `break` needed

✅ Add a new enum constant without a matching case? Compile error, not a silent bug at runtime

#Java #Java14 #CleanCode

💡 Share data between threads without `volatile` on every field.

✅ Thread A writes data, thread B reads a flag — B might see the flag change but still read stale data

✅ `volatile` forces every read and write to sync — safe, but heavier than you often need

✅ `setRelease` (publish) + `getAcquire` (subscribe): writer flips the flag when done; reader then sees all prior writes

💡 JUnit 5's Extension API replaces JUnit 4's `@RunWith` Runner and `@Rule`.

✅ One model: implement lifecycle hooks like `BeforeEachCallback` instead of an exclusive Runner

✅ `ParameterResolver` injects test dependencies — no base-class inheritance needed

✅ Extensions compose: stack several with `@ExtendWith` and they just work together

💡 Java tip💡: Use StringBuilder instead of String for concatenating strings in loops.

✅ StringBuilder is mutable and optimized for repeated string appends.

✅ Java tip💡: Use StringBuilder instead of String for concatenating strings in loops

✅ ❌ String is immutable, making it inefficient in loops, especially large ones

💡 Java tip💡: always close resources like BufferedReader, InputStream, OutputStream, using try-with-resources to avoid memory leaks.

enum DataScope {
    CUSTOMERS_READ,
    CUSTOMERS_WRITE,
    CUSTOMERS_UPDATE,
    CUSTOMERS_DELETE,
    ORDERS_READ,
    ORDERS_WRITE,
    ORDERS_UPDATE,
    ORDERS_DELETE,
    PRODUCTS_READ,
    PRODUCTS_WRITE,
    PRODUCTS_UPDATE,
    PRODUCTS_DELETE,
    INVENTORY_READ,
    INVENTORY_WRITE,
    INVENTORY_UPDATE,
    INVENTORY_DELETE,
    PAYMENTS_READ,
    PAYMENTS_WRITE,
    PAYMENTS_UPDATE,
    PAYMENTS_DELETE,
    SHIPMENTS_READ,
    SHIPMENTS_WRITE,
    SHIPMENTS_UPDATE,
    SHIPMENTS_DELETE,
    INVOICES_READ,
    INVOICES_WRITE,
    INVOICES_UPDATE,
    INVOICES_DELETE,
    REPORTS_READ,
    REPORTS_WRITE,
    REPORTS_UPDATE,
    REPORTS_DELETE,
    USERS_READ,
    USERS_WRITE,
    USERS_UPDATE,
    USERS_DELETE,
    ROLES_READ,
    ROLES_WRITE,
    ROLES_UPDATE,
    ROLES_DELETE,
    AUDIT_READ,
    AUDIT_WRITE,
    AUDIT_UPDATE,
    AUDIT_DELETE,
    CONFIG_READ,
    CONFIG_WRITE,
    CONFIG_UPDATE,
    CONFIG_DELETE,
}

// 48 flags — EnumSet: bit vector (~48 bits)
// HashSet: 48 entries + buckets + boxing overhead

// Build the full set
EnumSet<DataScope> admin = EnumSet.allOf(DataScope.class);
Set<DataScope> adminHash = new HashSet<>(Arrays.asList(DataScope.values()));

// Bulk change — bitwise OR/AND on a few machine words
EnumSet<DataScope> ordersDomain = EnumSet.of(
    DataScope.ORDERS_READ, DataScope.ORDERS_WRITE,
    DataScope.ORDERS_UPDATE, DataScope.ORDERS_DELETE);
admin.removeAll(ordersDomain);

EnumSet<DataScope> readOnly = EnumSet.copyOf(admin);
readOnly.removeIf(p -> !p.name().endsWith("_READ"));

// contains() — O(1) bit test, not hash + bucket walk
boolean ok = admin.contains(DataScope.PAYMENTS_READ);

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Disable console logging with one property.

✅ `logging.console.enabled=false`

✅ Useful in containers that only scrape files/OTLP

✅ Keep file or remote appenders as needed

#SpringBoot4 #Logging
code:
# application-prod.yml
logging:
  console:
    enabled: false
  file:
    name: /var/log/app/application.log
  level:
    root: INFO
    com.example: DEBUG

# Local profile keeps console on (default true)

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: JDK `HttpClient` uses virtual threads when enabled.

✅ Set `spring.threads.virtual.enabled=true`

✅ Auto-configured HTTP clients opt in

✅ Great for high-concurrency blocking I/O

#SpringBoot4 #VirtualThreads

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Built on Jakarta EE 11 + Servlet 6.1.

✅ JPA 3.2, Validation 3.1, Servlet 6.1

✅ Hibernate ORM 7.x on the classpath

✅ Verify container / server Servlet level before deploy

#SpringBoot4 #JakartaEE

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Elasticsearch API key authentication.

✅ New `spring.elasticsearch.api-key` property

✅ Cleaner than embedding basic auth in the URI

✅ Rotate keys without changing client code

#SpringBoot4 #Elasticsearch

🚀 Spring Boot 4: SSL health shows `expiringChains`.

✅ No more `WILL_EXPIRE_SOON` status value

✅ Expiring certs stay `VALID` + listed in details

✅ Tune `management.health.ssl.certificate-validity-warning-threshold`

#SpringBoot4 #SSL

🚀 Spring Boot 4: DevTools live reload is off by default.

✅ Restarts still work; browser live reload is opt-in

✅ Enable only when you need it locally

✅ Avoid surprise websocket ports in shared envs

#SpringBoot4 #DevTools
code:
<!-- optional local dependency -->
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-devtools</artifactId>
  <optional>true</optional>
</dependency>

# application-local.yml
spring:
  devtools:
    livereload:
      enabled: true
    restart:
      enabled: true

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: `EnvironmentPostProcessor` package moved.

✅ New: `org.springframework.boot.EnvironmentPostProcessor`

✅ Old package kept temporarily (deprecated)

✅ Update `spring.factories` / `AutoConfiguration.imports`

#SpringBoot4 #Config

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Exception translation property renamed.

✅ `spring.dao.exceptiontranslation.enabled` →

✅ `spring.persistence.exceptiontranslation.enabled`

✅ Use the properties migrator to catch it

#SpringBoot4 #Migration
code:
# ❌ Boot 3
spring:
  dao:
    exceptiontranslation:
      enabled: true

# ✅ Boot 4
spring:
  persistence:
    exceptiontranslation:
      enabled: true

// Still translates DataAccessException → @Repository proxies

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: `@MeterTag` SpEL on `@Timed` / `@Counted`.

✅ Tag metric series from method args

✅ Auto-config wires a SpEL `ValueExpressionResolver`

✅ Keep cardinality under control

#SpringBoot4 #Micrometer

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Support for `@ObservationKeyValue`.

✅ Attach key/values to Micrometer Observations

✅ Cleaner than manual `observation.highCardinalityKeyValue`

✅ Works with the OTLP starter export path

#SpringBoot4 #Observability

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Gradle 9 is supported.

✅ Build with Gradle 9 or 8.14+

✅ Keep the Boot Gradle plugin in sync with Boot 4

✅ Good moment to refresh toolchain + JVM args

#SpringBoot4 #Gradle

🚀 Spring Boot 4: AWS ECS is a recognized `CloudPlatform`.

✅ Detects Elastic Container Service runtime

✅ Aligns cloud property / actuator behavior

✅ Same idea as k8s / Cloud Foundry detection

#SpringBoot4 #AWS

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Control MongoDB `BigDecimal` storage.

✅ `spring.data.mongodb.representation.big-decimal`

✅ Avoid silent precision surprises

✅ Pick decimal128 vs string explicitly

#SpringBoot4 #MongoDB

🚀 Spring Boot 4: `@ServiceConnection` + MongoDB Atlas Local.

✅ Testcontainers `MongoDBAtlasLocalContainer` supported

✅ No hardcoded localhost ports in tests

✅ Boot wires connection details automatically

#SpringBoot4 #Testcontainers

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Prefer `JsonMapper` beans over `ObjectMapper`.

✅ Jackson 3 format-specific mappers

✅ Replacing JSON config? Define `JsonMapper`

✅ XML? Define `XmlMapper`

#SpringBoot4 #Jackson3

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Fully executable uber-jar launch scripts are gone.

✅ `java -jar app.jar` is the supported path

✅ Unix init-script packaging removed

✅ Use buildpacks, systemd, or Gradle application plugin

#SpringBoot4 #Packaging

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Upgrade path tip — go 3.5 → 4.0.

✅ Land on latest 3.5.x first

✅ Kill 3.x deprecations before the jump

✅ Then bump parent/BOM to 4.0.x and fix starters

#SpringBoot4 #Migration
code:
<!-- Step 1: latest 3.5.x, fix deprecations -->
<parent>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
  <version>3.5.16</version>
</parent>

<!-- Step 2: Boot 4 -->
<parent>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
  <version>4.0.7</version>
</parent>

<!-- Step 3: properties-migrator (temporary) + starter renames -->

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: HTTP Service Clients + `RestClient` under one roof.

✅ Interfaces for declarative calls

✅ `RestClient` for ad-hoc calls

✅ Shared `spring.http.clients` timeouts/SSL bundles

#SpringBoot4 #HttpExchange

🚀 Spring Boot 4: API versioning beans for advanced control.

✅ Provide `ApiVersionResolver`

✅ Optional `ApiVersionParser`

✅ `ApiVersionDeprecationHandler` for sunsetting

#SpringBoot4 #ApiVersioning

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Spring Security 7 comes along for the ride.

✅ Security filter chain DSL stays familiar

✅ Stronger crypto defaults — review customizers

✅ Use `spring-boot-starter-security` + `…-security-test`

#SpringBoot4 #SpringSecurity

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Tomcat static resource cache size is configurable.

✅ `server.tomcat.resource.cache-max-size`

✅ Tune when serving many static assets

✅ Defaults may surprise large SPAs

#SpringBoot4 #Tomcat
code:
# application.yml
server:
  tomcat:
    resource:
      cache-max-size: 10MB
  compression:
    enabled: true
    mime-types: text/html,text/xml,text/plain,application/json

spring:
  web:
    resources:
      chain:
        cache: true

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: Mark development tools in the jar manifest.

✅ `Spring-Boot-Jar-Type: development-tool`

✅ Keeps those deps out of production uber jars

✅ Useful for internal developer utilities

#SpringBoot4 #Packaging

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Milestones publish to Maven Central.

✅ No extra `repo.spring.io` for 4.x milestones

✅ Easier CI trials of M/RC builds

✅ Still pin versions explicitly in experiments

#SpringBoot4 #Maven
code:
<!-- try a milestone without custom repositories -->
<parent>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
  <version>4.1.0-M2</version>
</parent>

// Gradle: mavenCentral() is enough for Boot 4 milestones
repositories {
  mavenCentral()
}

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: Auto-config classes are no longer public API.

✅ Public members removed from auto-config types

✅ Don’t `@Import` auto-config classes directly

✅ Use starters + `AutoConfiguration.imports` only

#SpringBoot4 #AutoConfig

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Session Hazelcast/MongoDB starters removed.

✅ Ownership moved to Hazelcast / MongoDB teams

✅ Use their Spring Session modules directly

✅ Redis + JDBC session starters remain in Boot

#SpringBoot4 #Session

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Spock integration removed (Groovy 5).

✅ Spock isn’t ready for Groovy 5 yet

✅ Prefer JUnit 5 + AssertJ for Boot 4 tests

✅ Keep Spock suites on Boot 3.5 until Spock catches up

#SpringBoot4 #Testing

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Built on Spring Framework 7 — foundations matter.

✅ HTTP Service Clients + API versioning land in Boot

✅ JSpecify null-safety across the portfolio

✅ Plan starter renames + Jackson 3 before you upgrade

#SpringBoot4 #SpringFramework7
